questions.q: Siraj-ud-Daulah was the Nawab of which Indian region?
questions.explanation: Siraj-ud-Daulah was the Nawab of Bengal in the mid-18th century CE.

questions.q: Siraj-ud-Daulah is remembered as the last independent Nawab of Bengal before whose rise to power?
questions.explanation: His defeat marked a decisive step in the British East India Company's rise to political power in India.

questions.q: At which 1757 battle was Siraj-ud-Daulah decisively defeated?
questions.explanation: Siraj-ud-Daulah was defeated at the Battle of Plassey in 1757.

questions.q: In which century did Siraj-ud-Daulah confront the rising British power?
questions.explanation: Siraj-ud-Daulah ruled and was defeated in the mid-18th century CE.

questions.q: Which British commander defeated Siraj-ud-Daulah at Plassey?
questions.explanation: Robert Clive led the East India Company's forces against Siraj-ud-Daulah at Plassey.

questions.q: What did Siraj-ud-Daulah's defeat at Plassey give the British Company control over?
questions.explanation: The victory gave the Company control of Bengal's immense wealth and resources.

questions.q: The capital of Siraj-ud-Daulah's Bengal was located at which city?
questions.explanation: Murshidabad was the capital of the Nawabs of Bengal, including Siraj-ud-Daulah.

questions.q: Siraj-ud-Daulah came to the throne by succeeding which close relative?
questions.explanation: He succeeded his maternal grandfather, Nawab Alivardi Khan, in 1756.

questions.q: Whose defection during the battle was a major cause of Siraj-ud-Daulah's defeat at Plassey?
questions.explanation: The defection of his commander Mir Jafar was decisive in Siraj's defeat.

questions.q: Siraj-ud-Daulah was, in effect, a subordinate ruler nominally owing allegiance to which declining empire?
questions.explanation: The Nawabs of Bengal were nominally provincial governors under the declining Mughal Empire.

questions.q: In which year did Siraj-ud-Daulah become Nawab of Bengal?
questions.explanation: Siraj-ud-Daulah ascended as Nawab in 1756 on the death of Alivardi Khan.

questions.q: Which Calcutta fort did Siraj-ud-Daulah capture from the British in 1756, sparking the conflict that led to Plassey?
questions.explanation: Siraj seized Fort William and Calcutta from the Company in 1756, escalating the dispute.

questions.q: What name was given to the alleged confinement of British prisoners after Siraj captured Calcutta?
questions.explanation: British accounts described the 'Black Hole of Calcutta,' though the scale of the incident remains debated by historians.

questions.q: Siraj-ud-Daulah's grandfather Alivardi Khan had spent much of his reign resisting raids by which power?
questions.explanation: Alivardi Khan's reign was dominated by costly Maratha incursions into Bengal.

questions.q: What was the personal name of Siraj-ud-Daulah, 'Siraj-ud-Daulah' being a title?
questions.explanation: His personal name was Mirza Muhammad Siraj; 'Siraj-ud-Daulah' was an honorific title.

questions.q: Apart from Mir Jafar, which wealthy banking family is often said to have conspired against Siraj-ud-Daulah?
questions.explanation: The immensely rich Jagat Seth banking house is widely held to have backed the conspiracy against Siraj.

questions.q: Which two loyal commanders of Siraj fought hard at Plassey before being killed or sidelined?
questions.explanation: Mir Madan and Mohan Lal remained loyal and fought fiercely, but Mir Madan's death demoralised Siraj's army.

questions.q: After his defeat at Plassey, what was Siraj-ud-Daulah's fate?
options: He fled, was captured, and was executed
questions.explanation: Siraj fled after Plassey, was captured, and was put to death on the orders of Mir Jafar's faction.

questions.q: Who was installed as the new Nawab of Bengal by the British after Siraj-ud-Daulah's fall?
questions.explanation: The British rewarded Mir Jafar by making him the puppet Nawab of Bengal after Plassey.

questions.q: Siraj-ud-Daulah's reign as Nawab lasted only about how long before Plassey?
questions.explanation: He ruled barely a year, from 1756 to his defeat and death in 1757.

questions.q: One grievance Siraj-ud-Daulah held against the British concerned their abuse of which trade privilege?
questions.explanation: Siraj objected to the Company's misuse of dastaks, duty-free trade permits, which deprived Bengal of revenue.

questions.q: On what date was the Battle of Plassey, sealing Siraj-ud-Daulah's fate, fought?
questions.explanation: The Battle of Plassey was fought on 23 June 1757.

questions.q: Plassey (Palashi) lay on the banks of which river in Bengal?
questions.explanation: Palashi stood on the Bhagirathi-Hooghly, between Calcutta and Murshidabad.

questions.q: Which rival cousin had earlier contested Siraj's path to the throne, deepening factions in the Bengal court?
questions.explanation: Siraj's cousin Shaukat Jang, the faujdar of Purnea, challenged him before being defeated.

questions.q: A monsoon downpour during Plassey helped the British because it damaged whose gunpowder?
options: Siraj's, while the British covered theirs
The British, while Siraj covered his
questions.explanation: Rain spoiled the Nawab's unprotected gunpowder while the British kept theirs dry, blunting Siraj's artillery.

questions.q: Roughly how did the size of Siraj's army at Plassey compare with Clive's much smaller force?
questions.explanation: Siraj fielded tens of thousands against Clive's few thousand, yet treachery decided the outcome rather than numbers.

questions.q: Which French presence at the Bengal court alarmed the British and figured in the run-up to Plassey?
options: French artillerymen and the post at Chandernagore
questions.explanation: French gunners served Siraj and the British seized the French settlement of Chandernagore before Plassey.

questions.q: Who is traditionally named as the executioner or agent of Siraj-ud-Daulah's death after his capture?
options: Muhammad Ali Beg, on Miran's orders
questions.explanation: Siraj was killed on the orders of Mir Jafar's son Miran, the deed attributed to Muhammad Ali Beg.

questions.q: The fall of Siraj-ud-Daulah is often treated by historians as the beginning of British political dominance in which region first?
questions.explanation: Control of Bengal, Bihar, and Orissa flowed from Plassey, becoming the base of British power in India.

questions.q: In Bengali nationalist memory, Siraj-ud-Daulah is frequently portrayed as what kind of figure?
options: A tragic patriot betrayed by his own men
A weak puppet of the British
questions.explanation: Later Bengali tradition recast Siraj as a tragic patriot undone by betrayal, especially Mir Jafar's.
